message:
🔧 ACMM: auto-prune stale cards, level assignments, layout + UX fixes (#8433)

* 🔧 acmm: auto-prune stale cards + assign levels to all sources

Two fixes:

1. Auto-cleanup: when loading saved card layouts from localStorage,
   filter out card types that are no longer in the registry. This
   prevents ghost cards (like the removed acmm_balance) from
   persisting in users' browsers without manual localStorage clearing.

2. Level assignments: all 20 criteria from fullsend, AEF, and
   claude-reflect now have ACMM levels (2-5) so they participate in
   the lock/unlock gamification and show level badges in the inventory.

   L2: test-coverage, CI/CD, structural-gates, session-continuity
   L3: auto-merge, branch-protection, rollback-drill, task-traceability,
       change-classification, correction-capture, positive-reinforcement,
       preference-index, session-summary
   L4: production-feedback, observability-runbook, risk-assessment,
       audit-trail, cross-tool-config, claude-md-sync, reflection-review

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🎨 acmm: constrain feedback inventory to max-w-4xl

On the full-width (12w) card, the L2/ACMM badges and 'Ask agent for
help' button were pushed too far to the right, hard to see. Adding
max-w-4xl keeps the content readable regardless of card width.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 💡 acmm: add hint text on explore-level slider

'drag to preview AI vs human balance' — prompts users to interact
with the slider instead of wondering what it does.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+    <div className="h-full flex flex-col p-2 gap-2 max-w-4xl">
       <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5 flex-wrap">
         <Filter className="w-3.5 h-3.5 text-muted-foreground" />
         {sources.map((s) => (
